title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Will Not Power On
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table that will not power on.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the table is plugged into a known working outlet. Test the outlet with a voltmeter or another device. Reason: A dead or tripped outlet is a common, easily fixable cause.

steps.title: Check Circuit Breakers and Fuses
steps.instructions: Inspect wall or table circuit breakers. Reset if tripped. Check table-specific fuses if accessible externally. Reason: Overcurrent protection may prevent the table from powering.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord
steps.instructions: Look for cuts, fraying, or loose connections at both ends. Swap with a known good power cord if available. Reason: Damaged cords can prevent power without visible signs on the table.

steps.title: Examine Main Power Switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch on the table base is turned on. Toggle it off and on again. Reason: Sometimes the switch may not fully engage.

steps.title: Check Battery Backup (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Some Skytron 6500 tables have internal batteries for limited operation. Confirm battery is seated and charged, or bypass if external AC power is available. Reason: A dead backup battery may prevent control electronics from booting.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Look for any LEDs on the table base or pendant. No lights often indicate power not reaching internal electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Table will not power on; no lights or response from controls.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet functional, power cord intact, circuit breakers reset; table remained unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for vendor repair.

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Movement Functions Inoperative
description: Troubleshooting guide for Skytron 6500 Series surgical table when movement functions do not operate despite power.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is plugged into a live, dedicated outlet. Check for tripped breakers or blown fuses. Even if the table shows partial power (lights), movement motors require full voltage.

steps.title: Check Main Power Switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch is in the “ON” position. Some Skytron models have separate switches for power and motion enable. Movement functions may be disabled if the motion enable is off.

steps.title: Inspect Hand and Foot Controls
steps.instructions: Ensure all buttons are functioning and not stuck. Verify connection cables are fully seated. Swap with a known good pendant if available. Faulty or disconnected control units often mimic motor failure.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights or Error Codes
steps.instructions: Check display panel for error codes or flashing indicators. Document any patterns for repair escalation. Error codes can indicate motor drive or safety interlock issues.

steps.title: Check Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify table brakes are disengaged and base locks are not activated. Ensure any side rails or attachments are correctly positioned. Many Skytron tables will inhibit movement if safety conditions are not met.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Look for loose or damaged cables, including those connecting the power module to motors. Intermittent or damaged wiring can prevent movement.

steps.title: Test Motion Functions Individually
steps.instructions: Attempt raising/lowering, tilt, and lateral movements one at a time. Note any movement or sounds. Isolates which circuits or motors are affected.

CCR.Complaint: Table powers on but will not move in any direction.
CCR.Cause: All external checks performed; no movement observed from any control; no audible motor activity.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights normal or error codes documented
No unusual noises, heat, or odor observed
Table removed from service for repair

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Hand Control or Pendant Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting Skytron 6500 Series surgical table hand control or pendant that does not operate.

steps.title: Visual Inspection
steps.instructions: Check the hand control and pendant for physical damage, loose connectors, or frayed cables. Ensure the connector is fully seated in the table control port. Why: Loose or damaged connections are a frequent cause of control failure.

steps.title: Check Power and Table Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the table powers on and indicator lights on the table base are normal. Verify the table is not in a locked or maintenance mode that disables movement. Why: The hand control may appear nonfunctional if the table itself is not in an active mode.

steps.title: Cable Continuity Test
steps.instructions: If possible, use a multimeter to check the continuity of the pendant cable from the connector to the hand control. Swap the hand control with a known working one, if available. Why: Faulty wiring or internal cable breaks can prevent signal transmission.

steps.title: Battery Check (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some hand controls use internal batteries. Verify battery voltage and replace if low. Why: Weak or dead batteries can prevent button actuation or lights from functioning.

steps.title: Test Table Response
steps.instructions: With a verified working hand control or pendant, attempt all movement functions. Observe any movement or indicator response. Why: Confirms whether the issue is isolated to the control device or the table electronics.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff report that pressing buttons on the hand control or pendant does not move the table.
CCR.Cause: Visual inspection showed intact cabling; pendant does not respond; table powers on normally.
CCR.Resolution: Hand control/panel swapped with known working unit for verification. Table removed from service and sent for bench evaluation due to likely internal electronics failure.

helpfulDetails: Swapped with known working hand control

title: Skytron 6500 Series – Hydraulic Leaks or Slow Positioning
description: Guide for troubleshooting hydraulic issues on a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table, focusing on slow movement, leaks, and uneven operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Verify no patient is on the table. Move any patient to another table if necessary. Confirm table is not under load while troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Power and Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm table is properly plugged in and power switch is ON. Observe indicator lights to ensure system is active. Power cycling may reset some electronic flow controls.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control and Pendant
steps.instructions: Verify hand control or foot pendant functions normally. Unresponsive controls can mimic hydraulic slowness. Swap hand control with a known working unit if available.

steps.title: Check Hydraulic Fluid Level (External Reservoir if Accessible)
steps.instructions: Look for fluid sight glass or level indicators. Low fluid can cause slow or uneven movements. Top off only if safe and manufacturer-approved fluid is available.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Hydraulic Lines and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect hoses, fittings, and visible cylinders for leaks or damage. Look for wet spots, drips, or fluid stains. Tighten loose fittings carefully without forcing connections.

steps.title: Observe Table Movement Under No Load
steps.instructions: Test each movement (height, tilt, lateral) individually. Note any slow response or irregular motion. Listen for abnormal hissing, groaning, or jerky action.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Ensure base and lift mechanisms are free of debris. Verify cables or other items are not pinched, restricting cylinder travel.

CCR.Complaint: Table moves slowly during height and tilt adjustments; visible hydraulic fluid under base.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed minor leaks at a cylinder fitting and low fluid in reservoir.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for authorized repair.

helpfulDetails: Location of leak and fluid color/type
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Skytron 6500 Series - Table Drift or Position Instability
description: Guide to troubleshoot unintended table drift or inability to maintain position on a Skytron 6500 Series surgical table.

steps.title: Confirm Patient Safety and Table Load
steps.instructions: Ensure no patient is on the table. Remove all accessories or weight plates that may affect movement. Why: Drift can be exaggerated under load; confirming an unloaded table isolates mechanical/hydraulic issues.

steps.title: Check Power and Indicator Lights
steps.instructions: Verify the table is properly plugged in and breaker is on. Confirm main power indicator lights are steady and no error codes are displayed. Why: Inconsistent power can cause control or drift behavior.

steps.title: Inspect Foot and Hand Controls
steps.instructions: Disconnect and reconnect hand and foot pendants. Check for stuck buttons, damage, or moisture. Why: A partially stuck or malfunctioning switch can send unintended movement signals.

steps.title: Check Table Leveling and Casters
steps.instructions: Ensure floor surface is even and casters are locked. Why: Unlevel support can create the appearance of drift when it is mechanical.

steps.title: Inspect Hydraulic Lines and Connections
steps.instructions: Visually inspect under the table for leaks or loose fittings. Look for wet spots, worn hoses, or damaged seals. Why: Hydraulic leaks reduce pressure and allow table segments to drift.

steps.title: Test Individual Table Functions
steps.instructions: Cycle each motion (height, tilt, lateral, Trendelenburg) individually without load. Note if drift occurs in a specific axis or all motions. Why: Isolating functions can indicate if the drift is system-wide or limited to one actuator.

steps.title: Check for Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure temperature and room conditions are within manufacturer specs. Check for electrical interference or nearby equipment that could affect electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Table drifts when set in Trendelenburg position; cannot maintain height.
CCR.Cause: No external issues found; hand controls and power verified; hydraulic lines visually intact.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Verify outlet voltage and breaker condition
Document hand and foot control response
Note any unusual sounds or heat from hydraulic motors
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Skytron Aurora Surgical Light - Light Head Fails to Power On
description: Guide to troubleshoot a Skytron Aurora surgical light head that fails to power on, focusing on older wiring or transformer issues.

steps.title: Verify Power Availability
steps.instructions: Confirm the room or circuit supplying the light is energized. Check for tripped breakers or blown fuses. Why: Ensures the issue isn’t simply a loss of facility power.

steps.title: Inspect Wall Switch or Control Panel
steps.instructions: Operate the on/off switch or control interface. Observe any response from the light head (LED indicators, humming). Why: Confirms the signal to the light head is reaching it.

steps.title: Check Visible Wiring Connections
steps.instructions: Examine accessible wiring between the ceiling mount, transformer, and light head for loose connections or obvious damage. Ensure connectors are seated fully and not corroded. Why: Older wiring can develop loose or oxidized connections that prevent operation.

steps.title: Inspect Transformer or Power Supply Module
steps.instructions: If accessible, verify the transformer is securely connected and shows normal indicators (if any). Look for signs of overheating, burn marks, or unusual smells. Why: The transformer provides the correct voltage; faults here commonly prevent power-on.

steps.title: Swap with Known-Good Circuit or Transformer (if possible)
steps.instructions: Temporarily connect the light head to a verified functional power source. Why: Helps isolate whether the issue is in the light head or upstream wiring/transformer.

steps.title: Test Light Head Independently (Optional External Test)
steps.instructions: If the design allows, remove the head and connect to an alternative verified supply per manufacturer safety guidelines. Why: Confirms whether the light head itself is functional.

CCR.Complaint: Light head fails to power on; no illumination during operation.
CCR.Cause: External inspection shows loose transformer wiring and possible degraded connector; no visible signs of light head failure.
CCR.Resolution: Secured wiring connections; light tested with alternate power supply; issue persists. Light removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual smells or heat from transformer

title: Skytron Aurora Surgical Light - Arm Articulation or Balance Drift
description: Guide for troubleshooting Skytron Aurora surgical light arm drift or sag, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify the Environment and Mounting
steps.instructions: Ensure the ceiling or floor mount is secure and level. Check that no external obstruction (equipment, drapes) is interfering with the light arm. Why: A poorly secured mount can mimic balance drift or sag.

steps.title: Inspect Arm Joints for Looseness
steps.instructions: Gently move the arm through its full range of motion. Look for wobble, uneven movement, or excessive play at joints. Why: Loose pivot points reduce stability and cause drift.

steps.title: Check Tension Mechanisms
steps.instructions: Locate external tension adjustment screws or knobs on the arm (if accessible). Verify the arm resists movement slightly but can still be positioned smoothly. Why: Over time, springs or tension mechanisms can weaken, causing sagging.

steps.title: Look for Wear or Damage on External Components
steps.instructions: Inspect arm covers, joints, and visible bearings for cracks, corrosion, or missing parts. Why: Physical damage can impair balance even if tension is correct.

steps.title: Test Light Head Weight and Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove any added equipment or handles mounted on the light head. Reposition the arm to see if drift improves. Why: Extra weight can affect balance beyond designed limits.

steps.title: Verify Manufacturer Adjustment Procedure
steps.instructions: Some Aurora models have external spring tension adjustments or locking mechanisms. Carefully follow manufacturer guidance for safe adjustments if indicated. Why: Correct tension is critical for proper articulation.

CCR.Complaint: Light arm drifts downward when positioned; cannot hold set height.
CCR.Cause: Arm joints show slight wobble and tension spring adjustment appears weak; no external obstructions detected.
CCR.Resolution: Verified arm mounting and joints; attempted external tension adjustment per manufacturer guidelines; issue persists. Device removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Ceiling or floor mount stability checked
No unusual sounds noted during movement
Final device status: Out of Service, sent for repair

title: Skytron Aurora Surgical Light – LED Module Burnout or Uneven Light Spots
description: Guide to troubleshoot burned-out LEDs or uneven illumination on Skytron Aurora surgical lights.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the light is properly connected to a verified, functioning outlet. Check for blown fuses, tripped breakers, or loose plug connections. Why: LED modules may appear dim or uneven if the light isn’t receiving proper voltage.

steps.title: Inspect Light Head for Visible Damage
steps.instructions: Look for cracked lenses, discoloration, or loose LED modules. Check for signs of overheating or physical stress. Why: Physical damage can create shadowing or uneven light output.

steps.title: Clean Lenses and Reflectors
steps.instructions: Use a manufacturer-approved, non-abrasive cleaner to remove dust, fingerprints, or debris. Why: Contamination can make the light appear uneven even if LEDs are functional.

steps.title: Check LED Module Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure connectors between the LED modules and driver boards are seated properly. Why: Loose or partially connected modules can cause dim or non-functioning spots.

steps.title: Perform Light Pattern Test
steps.instructions: Turn on the light and observe the light field for uneven spots or dead LEDs. Compare against other functioning Aurora lights, if available. Why: Confirms whether the problem is isolated to specific LEDs or general illumination.

steps.title: Swap Modules (if available and serviceable)
steps.instructions: If a spare LED module exists, carefully replace a suspect module following manufacturer guidelines. Why: Helps confirm whether uneven lighting is due to a specific module or broader system issue.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical staff reported uneven light spots and some non-functioning LEDs during procedures.
CCR.Cause: Inspection revealed one or more burned-out LED modules; all power connections verified.
CCR.Resolution: Light removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for LED module replacement/bench repair.

helpfulDetails: Light pattern tested for uneven spots
Final device status: Out of Service, pending repair

title: Skytron Aurora Surgical Light - Wall / Remote Control Panel Communication Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting communication errors between Aurora surgical light and wall or remote control panels, focusing on external checks before internal board assessment.

steps.title: Verify Power to Light Head and Control Panels
steps.instructions: Check that the surgical light is powered on. Verify that the wall or remote control panel is receiving power (indicator LEDs on the panel). Why it matters: Communication errors can appear if either device is unpowered.

steps.title: Inspect Connection Cables
steps.instructions: Check the communication cable between the light head and wall/remote panel for loose connections, fraying, or visible damage. Reseat connectors on both ends if applicable. Why it matters: Loose or damaged cables are a common cause of intermittent communication errors.

steps.title: Check Network or Bus Wiring (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: For lights using data buses (RS-485, CAN, or proprietary), ensure continuity with a cable tester or visual inspection. Confirm no junction boxes or splitters are damaged or disconnected. Why it matters: Broken or improperly terminated data lines can prevent panel commands from reaching the light head.

steps.title: Test Alternate Control Panel (If Available)
steps.instructions: Swap the wall or remote panel with another known-working unit. Why it matters: Isolates whether the error is with the light head or the panel.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Turn off the light and panels for 30 seconds, then power back on. Why it matters: Resets temporary communication faults in electronics.

steps.title: Observe Error Indicators
steps.instructions: Check the light head for any diagnostic LEDs or error codes. Note if the error is intermittent or constant. Why it matters: Provides clues about whether the problem is systemic or isolated to one panel.

CCR.Complaint: Wall control panel intermittently unresponsive; error code displayed on Aurora surgical light.
CCR.Cause: Communication cable between light head and panel was loose; power cycling did not resolve issue completely.
CCR.Resolution: Cable resecured, system tested; if issue recurs, panel or light head controller board requires service.

helpfulDetails: Power indicators on panels and light head
Cable condition and connection points checked
Error codes or LED indicators noted

title: Skytron Aurora Surgical Light - Intensity Adjustment Knob or Switch Malfunction
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive or erratic intensity control on Skytron Aurora surgical lights, focusing on external checks before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Power to the Light
steps.instructions: Confirm the light is powered on at the main switch. Check circuit breakers and outlet power. Why: Intensity adjustment cannot function if the light is not receiving consistent power.

steps.title: Inspect the Knob or Switch Mechanism
steps.instructions: Ensure the physical knob is not loose, cracked, or damaged. Rotate the knob fully and check for smooth movement. Why: Mechanical failure can prevent proper contact in the switch.

steps.title: Check for Lockout or Safety Modes
steps.instructions: Confirm the light is not in a preset or locked intensity mode via wall or remote panel. Why: Some Aurora models have user or procedure presets that override manual adjustment.

steps.title: Verify Wall/Remote Panel Functionality
steps.instructions: If a remote panel is used, check for response from the panel. Swap with a known-working panel if available. Why: Communication errors may mimic a malfunctioning knob.

steps.title: Examine Wiring and Connectors
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the knob or switch wiring for loose connections or obvious damage. Ensure connectors are seated properly. Why: Intermittent connection can cause erratic intensity behavior.

steps.title: Test Adjustment Response
steps.instructions: Cycle intensity from minimum to maximum and observe light response. Note if the response is gradual, jumps, or fails entirely. Why: Patterns of failure can indicate switch, wiring, or control board issues.

CCR.Complaint: Intensity knob on Aurora surgical light is unresponsive or erratic.
CCR.Cause: Observed loose knob, unresponsive to rotation; wall panel also fails to change intensity.
CCR.Resolution: Inspected connections and wiring; found no external fix. Light removed from service and sent for repair.

title: Skytron Stellar Surgical Light - Light Head Fails to Power On (Electronics/Controller Faults)
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot Stellar surgical light heads that fail to power on due to electronics or controller issues, focusing on external checks before internal repair.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the light is properly connected to the electrical supply. Check the circuit breaker or fuse supplying the surgical light. Confirm outlet voltage with a multimeter. Why it matters: No power or low voltage can mimic electronic failure.

steps.title: Inspect External Cables and Connectors
steps.instructions: Check power cables for visible damage or loose connections. Inspect any modular connectors between the control module and light head. Re-seat connectors to ensure proper contact. Why it matters: Loose or damaged connectors are a common, easily fixed cause of power loss.

steps.title: Check Wall or Remote Control Panel Functionality
steps.instructions: Confirm the wall/remote control panel powers on and responds correctly. Observe for error codes or unresponsive buttons. Why it matters: Controller faults can prevent the light head from receiving activation commands.

steps.title: Cycle Power
steps.instructions: Power down the light system completely for 1–2 minutes. Power it back on and observe any status indicators. Why it matters: Electronics may require a reset to clear temporary faults.

steps.title: Inspect Indicator LEDs
steps.instructions: Look for any fault or warning LEDs on the control module. Note blinking sequences or colors and compare with manufacturer documentation if available. Why it matters: Indicator LEDs can help identify whether the fault is in the controller or the light head.

steps.title: Swap or Test Controller Module (if available)
steps.instructions: If the facility has a spare controller module compatible with Stellar, swap to determine if the controller is defective. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is in the light head or the controller electronics.

CCR.Complaint: Light head fails to power on; control panel unresponsive.
CCR.Cause: Power verified; cables and connectors secure; wall/remote panel functional; controller module may be faulty.
CCR.Resolution: Light removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Cable inspection: No damage, fully seated
Control panel indicators: Any error codes noted
Final device status: Out of Service

title: Skytron Stellar Surgical Light - Suspension Arm Drift or Sensor Misalignment
description: Guide for troubleshooting Skytron Stellar surgical light suspension arm drift or sensor misalignment, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm no procedure is active under the affected light. Move the patient or surgical field to an alternative light if needed.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Arm Mechanics
steps.instructions: Check for obvious obstructions, wear, or damage to the suspension arm joints. Look for loose screws, bolts, or brackets that may affect balance.

steps.title: Check Arm Tension Settings
steps.instructions: Locate any tension adjustment knobs or springs on the arm. Adjust according to manufacturer specifications to see if drift is corrected. This step matters because improper tension is a common cause of drift.

steps.title: Sensor Alignment Check
steps.instructions: Inspect position or tilt sensors for obvious misalignment or obstruction. Clean sensors gently with a dry cloth to remove dust or debris. Misaligned sensors can cause automatic drift correction to malfunction.

steps.title: Test Arm Movement
steps.instructions: Slowly move the arm through its full range of motion. Observe for smooth operation and if the arm holds position without drifting. Listen for unusual friction sounds or catches that indicate mechanical wear.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Light
steps.instructions: Turn the light off and back on after mechanical adjustments. Some sensor-based positioning systems require a reboot to recalibrate.

CCR.Complaint: Light arm drifts downward or does not hold position; sensor may appear misaligned.
CCR.Cause: Loose tension adjustment or obstruction/misalignment of suspension arm sensor.
CCR.Resolution: Inspected and adjusted tension; cleaned and realigned sensor; tested arm motion; device continues to drift → sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Arm movement smoothness and any unusual sounds
Final device status: sent for service if unresolved

title: Skytron Stellar Surgical Light - LED Module or Cluster Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for burned-out LED modules or clusters in Skytron Stellar surgical lights, focusing on external checks before assuming internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Power Supply
steps.instructions: Confirm the light head is connected to a powered outlet. Check breaker panels and ensure the circuit is active. Why: LED failure may appear as module burnout when the light simply isn’t receiving power.

steps.title: Check Main Control Interface
steps.instructions: Operate the light using wall-mounted control panels or hand controls. Ensure brightness settings are at an adequate level. Why: Communication errors may mimic LED failure.

steps.title: Inspect LED Indicators
steps.instructions: Observe any error codes or warning lights on the light head. Note patterns of LEDs that do not illuminate. Why: Identifies whether failure is isolated to specific clusters.

steps.title: Visual Inspection of Light Head
steps.instructions: Look for physical damage, discoloration, or smoke residue. Inspect connectors for secure seating. Why: Loose connectors can cause cluster failures.

steps.title: Swap or Test Light Head Modules
steps.instructions: If the system allows, replace the suspected LED module or cluster with a known good one. Why: Confirms whether the failure is module-specific or systemic.

steps.title: Check Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure room temperature and ventilation are adequate. Why: Overheating can prematurely damage LEDs.

steps.title: Review Manufacturer Logs (if available)
steps.instructions: Check for LED error codes or alerts logged in the Stellar control software. Why: Helps confirm the scope and location of failure.

CCR.Complaint: Light head shows dark or uneven illumination; some LED clusters not illuminating.
CCR.Cause: Visual inspection confirmed LED cluster failure; power and control inputs verified good.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to manufacturer for module replacement.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual heat or discoloration noted.
Final device status: Out of Service, pending LED module replacement.

title: Skytron Stellar Surgical Light – Touchscreen or Digital Control Panel Faults
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive, erratic, or error-displaying touchscreens or digital control panels on Skytron Stellar surgical lights, focusing on external checks before internal electronics failure.

steps.title: Ensure Power and Connections
steps.instructions:
Verify the light is properly connected to a live electrical outlet.
Check that all main power switches are on.
Confirm no tripped breakers or blown fuses are present.
Why: A completely unpowered system can mimic touchscreen or panel failure.

steps.title: Inspect Control Panel for Physical Damage
steps.instructions:
Look for cracks, water intrusion, or obvious debris on the touchscreen.
Check that mounting connectors are secure.
Why: Physical damage or loose connectors can prevent proper panel response.

steps.title: Clean Touchscreen or Panel
steps.instructions:
Use manufacturer-approved cleaning solution and lint-free cloth.
Remove any residue, fingerprints, or liquids that may interfere with capacitive touch.
Why: Contamination can cause erratic or non-responsive behavior.

steps.title: Check for Panel Initialization or Boot Issues
steps.instructions:
Power cycle the surgical light (turn off, wait 30 seconds, turn on).
Observe if the panel completes boot-up correctly and responds to input.
Why: Digital panels may fail temporarily due to software hang or startup glitch.

steps.title: Inspect Cable Connections to Control Module
steps.instructions:
If accessible, ensure ribbon cables or data connectors between the panel and light controller are fully seated.
Look for visible pin damage or corrosion.
Why: Poor connections can result in partial or total panel failure.

steps.title: Test Basic Functionality with Backup Controls
steps.instructions:
If the light has a secondary control method (wall panel, footswitch, or manual knob), verify the light responds through that interface.
Why: Confirms whether the issue is isolated to the touchscreen/panel.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported that the Stellar touchscreen does not respond or shows error messages when adjusting light intensity and position.
CCR.Cause: Upon inspection, the touchscreen was unresponsive after multiple power cycles; no physical damage was evident, and secondary controls worked intermittently.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service, labeled “Out of Service,” and scheduled for manufacturer repair of the digital control panel.

helpfulDetails: Device temperature or unusual sounds: Note if observed

title: Skytron Stellar Surgical Light – Light Intensity Programming Not Responding
description: Guide for troubleshooting unresponsive intensity programming on Skytron Stellar surgical lights, focusing on external causes before internal electronics.

steps.title: Verify Power to Light
steps.instructions: Ensure the light head and control panels are receiving power. Check for illuminated indicators on the light and panel. Confirm that the wall outlet or circuit is functional using a known working device. Why: Power interruptions can mimic programming faults.

steps.title: Check Control Panel Functionality
steps.instructions: Tap the touchscreen or press buttons on the digital panel to confirm responsiveness. Observe for delayed or erratic behavior. If multiple panels exist (wall, pendant, mobile), test all to rule out panel-specific issues. Why: Faulty panels can prevent intensity programming from executing.

steps.title: Inspect Connections and Cabling
steps.instructions: Verify that cables between the light head, control panel, and power source are secure. Look for pinched, damaged, or loose connectors. Why: Communication loss between components can block programming commands.

steps.title: Reset the Light Head / Control Panel
steps.instructions: Follow facility-approved procedure for a power cycle or soft reset of the light. Observe if intensity programming responds after reset. Why: Temporary software glitches often clear with a reset.

steps.title: Review Programming Mode / Presets
steps.instructions: Check that the light is in the correct mode for programmable intensity control. Confirm presets or saved settings are not locked or overridden. Why: Improper mode selection can prevent adjustments from taking effect.

steps.title: Observe for Alarms or Error Messages
steps.instructions: Note any error codes or panel messages. Document these for repair or manufacturer consultation if needed.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical light intensity programming is unresponsive and presets will not adjust brightness.
CCR.Cause: Power and connections verified, panels tested and responsive, reset performed, but intensity programming remains nonfunctional. No external faults found.
CCR.Resolution: Light removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service
